description: Background: You are acting as a DevSecOps engineer who has to enforce “policy-as-code” in an existing repository. The security policy states that PermitRootLogin must never be set to yes in any SSH server configuration file. Current repository layout (already present when the session starts): /home/user/repo/ └── config/ ├── sshd_config └── sshd_config_alt Requirement (single-step automation): 1. Search every file beneath /home/user/repo/config (recursively) for the exact string PermitRootLogin yes. 2. Write **only the matching lines** to the file /home/user/compliance/root_login_findings.log using exactly this output format, one finding per line: FULL_PATH_TO_FILE:LINE_NUMBER:MATCHED_LINE_TEXT • FULL_PATH_TO_FILE must be the absolute path to the file that contains the finding. • LINE_NUMBER must be the 1-based line number where the string occurs. • MATCHED_LINE_TEXT must be the complete line text as it appears in the file (leading/trailing whitespace trimmed). 3. If no findings are discovered the file must still be created but left **completely empty** (size 0 bytes). Constraints: • Accomplish everything with a single shell command (pipelines are allowed, but wrap them in one command line). • Do NOT modify any file under /home/user/repo/. • Make sure the directory /home/user/compliance/ exists or create it if needed; you do not have sudo rights. The automated test will verify: a) that /home/user/compliance/root_login_findings.log exists, b) that its contents exactly match the format described above, c) that only lines containing PermitRootLogin yes are present. Produce the file and return to the prompt. No other output is necessary.
truth:
Initial file system state (before the agent starts):

1. /home/user/repo/config/sshd_config  
   Exact contents with line numbers:  
     1 # OpenSSH server configuration  
     2 Port 22  
     3 Protocol 2  
     4 PasswordAuthentication yes  
     5 # allow root login  
     6 PermitRootLogin yes  
     7 ChallengeResponseAuthentication no  

2. /home/user/repo/config/sshd_config_alt  
   Exact contents with line numbers:  
     1 # Alternative config  
     2 Port 2222  
     3 Protocol 2  
     4 PermitRootLogin no  

3. No directory /home/user/compliance/ exists at start.

Expected correct artefact after the agent finishes:

File: /home/user/compliance/root_login_findings.log  
Permissions: 0644 (agent’s default umask is acceptable)  
Expected content (including final newline character):

/home/user/repo/config/sshd_config:6:PermitRootLogin yes

—that is exactly 1 line, ending in a single newline, with no extra spaces before or after any field.

If the agent mistakenly reports any other lines, has wrong formatting, or does not create the file, tests must fail.
name: task_000000_bb16b908
